strengths: log.md seed-retention claim matches committed POINTS exactly (verified by hand)
determinism claim 'restart 0 = 18, restart 4 = 19' reproduced live with seed=20260420
prime selection rationale (p=11 vs p=13) is explicit, correct, and visualized
single self-contained solution.py with no-collinear-triple asserts; pinned RNG seed
concerns: no behavior.gif or trace.csv — slightly thinner artifact set than primary
end-to-end reproducibility of 300+ restarts claimed but not re-run

strengths: richer artifact set: generator (376 lines), per-point sources file, trace.csv, behavior.gif
two parameterized QR curves — broader construction space than axis-swap
concerns: log.md source-label counts (7/1/8/4) disagree with solution_sources.py (6/1/8/5)
'plateaus at 16 without swap' tied to count (4) that disagrees with 5
figure (c) color-coded counts must match solution_sources.py (not log.md prose)

winner_reason: r1's claims (seed retention count, restart-number milestones, p=11 vs p=13 rationale) are exactly verifiable and were confirmed live; primary's log.md source-label counts (7/1/8/4) disagree with its own solution_sources.py (6/1/8/5).
cross_replica_inconsistencies: Both solutions are valid 20-point configurations with 2-per-row, 2-per-column layout. Direct overlap is 2/20; max overlap under any of 8 grid symmetries is 6/20 — structurally distinct.
Primary uses two free QR curves with (a,b,c) parameterization; r1 uses a fixed QR curve y=x^2 mod 11 plus axis-swap. Neither matches canonical Flammenkamp tabulations (no literature plagiarism).
